---
slug: "web-development-dictionary"
title: "Web Development Dictionary"
subtitle: "Key Buzzwords Commonly Used in Website Design and Development"
published_date: "2022-09-12"
published_date_raw: "12 Sep, 2022"
published_date_day: "12"
published_date_month: "Sep"
category_slug: "website"
category_title: "Website"
author_slug: "scott-green"
author_id: 2
hero_image: "/uploads/blog/web-development-dictionary/hero.jpg"
hero_alt: ""
thumbnail: "/uploads/blog/web-development-dictionary/thumbnail.jpg"
thumbnail_alt: ""
brief: "Have you listened to a conversation between people in the tech field? And even though you speak the same language, you just don't understand a word? Well, that’s because there is a certain common lingo used in the tech world (like all fields of work) that you wouldn’t know unless you are exposed to them. The world of technology is rife with peculiar terms and buzzwords, and this list is constantly growing."
meta_title: "Web Development Dictionary"
meta_desc: "Have you listened to a conversation between people in the tech field? And even though you speak the same language, you just don't understand a word? Well, that’s because..."
original_url: "https://ahdesign.website/post/web-development-dictionary"
body_html: |
  <p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Have you listened to a conversation between people in the tech field? And even though you speak the same language, you just don't understand a word? Well, that’s because there is a certain common lingo used in the tech world (like all fields of work) that you wouldn’t know unless you are exposed to them. The world of technology is rife with peculiar terms and buzzwords, and this list is constantly growing.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">When it comes down to it though, grasping a basic understanding of these words is relatively simple. But when many of these words are put into a sentence, it’s a little trickier. </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">We know it can be difficult and daunting, and this is why we’ve created this dictionary.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">301 redirect</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">A 301 redirect is as the name suggests, a redirection. You may want your website URL to be https://greensgarden.com, which is fine, but you will also have https://www.greensgarden.com. 301 redirect is a permanent server-side redirection to your preferred URL, this is done so there is only one version of your website out there.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">404</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">This can be called “Error 404,” “HTTP 404,” “404 error,” or “Error 404 not found.” They all mean the same thing which is just that the browser was able to communicate with the server, but the server was unable to find the requested site. A simple refresh usually fixes this.</span></p><h4><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></h4><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Alt Text</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Alt text is a type of written copy that displays where the images on your website are supposed to be if they don’t load. The text will disappear when the image loads. It is a good idea to have Alt Text as it helps with SEO and when people are using screen readers.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Backend</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">The backend of the website is the part that few people ever see, and is never seen by the general public. It is what’s called server-side (see below) and is responsible for the functioning of the website, its storage, organization, and manipulation of data.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Bounce Rate</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Refers to the percentage of visitors that come to your website, but don’t actually do anything. They visit your homepage or wherever they were directed and they don’t go any further. This is sometimes due to poor design, and slow loading, amongst other things.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Browser</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Simply put a browser is Google Chrome. It’s a piece of software that allows us to interpret and access the internet. Google Chrome isn’t the only browser though, there are others like Mozilla Firefox, Opera, Microsoft Edge, and other derivatives of these.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Bug</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">A bug isn’t an ant or likewise, it is an error in the code. This bug causes issues and prevents the website/ web app (see below) from functioning correctly.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Cache</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Is a box that collects and stores temporary data for websites, you won’t notice it, but it actually helps speed up the loading of websites as some of the information is already stored on your browser.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Cloud</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">A data storage system where files are saved to a server and not individual computers, and therefore accessible from any computer when your credentials are entered.</span></p><h4><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></h4><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">CMS</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Content Management System is software for your website that is used to edit the content all over your website. It can also be used to upload, schedule, and publish articles.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Code</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Often referred to as program code, it is a type of language (see below) that developers use to build websites, programs, etc that computers can understand.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Cookies</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Are a file created by the website and stored in your browser that enables the website to load faster,</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Crawl</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">This is what search engines like Google do to your website. It’s essentially just sending someone (not a real person though) to your website to search it, and gather information on what you offer, as well as what pages you have. This is so they can update the database directly affecting your </span><a href="https://ahdesign.website/post/seo-for-beginners" rel="noopener noreferrer" style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(17, 85, 204);" target="_blank">SEO</a><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);"> ranking.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">CTA</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">A call to action is a message placed strategically on your website or social media accounts and is a call for your visitors to do something, these messages are usually: </span><strong style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Try for Free, Register Now, Click for More Information.</strong></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Debugging</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Remember the bugs we mentioned earlier, well now has come the time to fix them. Identify what is causing the bug and apply a fix so it’s no longer an issue.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Domain Name</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">The address you enter into your browser to find and enter a website.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Encryption</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Encryption is the process of some or all of a user's activity on the internet being protected from others easily viewing what they’re doing.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Favicon</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">The icon that appears on the left side of the browser tab, it’s short for “favorite icon.” They’re used to make the tab seem more appealing and quickly distinguishable from the others.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Firewall</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">A firewall is a proverbial wall that scans traffic trying to enter websites, it can either allow it to pass or block it from entering. Depending on what you are using your website for, firewalls are a must.</span></p><h4><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></h4><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Frontend</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">The opposite of the backend. The frontend of the website is what you can see and access.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">FTP</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">File Transfer Protocol is a way for files to be uploaded, downloaded, and transferred through the internet. These transfers can either be from computer to computer, or they can go through the cloud.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Fullstack</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Refers to developers who are accomplished in working in both backend and frontend web development.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Git</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">One of the most important things to many developers, Git is a system where developers can store and manage the code they write. Git gives developers version control, and the ability to track changes and reverse them if needed.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">GitHub</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Is the same as Git, in the respect that it’s a cloud interface for Git. But it also offers other features not available in the original.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">GUI</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">The graphical user interface is the face of the program you use. All the buttons, icons, and everything else is part of the GUI. If there was no GUI, you would be doing everything in code. This interface makes the program usable for the general user.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Hex</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Hex Color Codes are actually something quite tricky to fully understand, but in short, Hex stands for hexadecimal and refers to a 16-digit system of classifying colors in a way that can be easily understood by computers. All colors are represented by the hex system and the color code will look something like this: #ff0000. </span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">HTTP</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Hypertext Transfer Protocol is how servers and browsers communicate by transferring data across the internet. For example, HTTP sends an image file from an HTTP server (webserver) which is then sent to an HTTP client program (web browser.)</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">HTTPS</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure is essentially the same as above, except that it is an HTTP extension and allows for secure communication across the internet.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Landing Page</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Not to be confused with the homepage, a landing page is quite different. Landing pages are to support a campaign by the marketing team and are usually a Call to Action (see above.) In short landing pages help to pique visitors' interest and convert these visitors into clients.</span></p><h4><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></h4><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Languages</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Programming languages are no different from the languages we all speak. They are a way of communicating with computers and telling them what exactly it is you want them to do, or a certain program to do. Below are some of the most common computing languages.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Meta tags and metadata</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">There are different types of metadata, but in the end, all types of metadata are just data that provides information about other data. This metadata is just a shorthand representation of the data it is referring to, also known as meta tags. These meta tags are not displayed on the page, nor seen by the user, they’re there for search engines to read.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">MVP</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Minimum Viable Product is quite a common buzzword in many fields and refers to a product that is typically made quickly and fairly barebones. This program is made with a minimal amount of services for it to still function viably, with the intention of gathering data from market needs. Then features are added, and the program is finished as per the needs identified by the MVP.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Open-source</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Indicates software that the original source code is available to see, modify, and redistribute freely. Open-source software is preferred by many users due to the availability of the code to be read, scrutinized, and understood. GIMP, LibreOffice, and GNULinux and some of the many examples of open-source software.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Plugin</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Plugins are software extensions that allow users to customize and add extra features to applications. Plugins are usually created because the original program didn’t offer this feature, so someone built it and offers it as a plugin.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">SaaS</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Software as a Service refers to a distribution model of software in which specific cloud (see above) providers host applications from their servers, thus making the application available to end users. Examples of SaaS are Dropbox, Office 365, Slack, and Canva.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Alongside SaaS you may also hear of:</span></p><ul><li><span style="background-color: transparent;">PaaS (Platform as a Service)</span></li><li><span style="background-color: transparent;">IaaS (Infrastructure as a Service)</span></li></ul><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Which are the same as SaaS except that they offer their respective service (platform or infrastructure) in the same model.</span></p><p><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">SEO</span></h4><p><a href="https://ahdesign.website/post/seo-for-beginners" rel="noopener noreferrer" style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(17, 85, 204);" target="_blank">Search Engine Optimization</a><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);"> is the practice of increasing the amount of traffic to websites. It can be done by writing specific copy, using internal and outbound links, keywords, and much more.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">SERP</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Search Engine Results Page is very similar to SEO in the way that SEO improves the findability of websites and SERP is the result pages presented to users. Having good SEO practices and standards ensures that you are high up in the SERPs.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Sitemap</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">A sitemap isn’t a guide for users to understand where to find things on your website, it’s a file used by search engines that provides information on pages, videos, and other things on websites.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">SSL Certificate</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Is a digital security certificate that authenticates the identity of websites to the users' browser and allows for an encrypted (see above) connection to happen.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">SVG</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Scalable Vector Graphics is an image format that allows for a greater amount of uses. In traditional image formats like JPG, the image is based on pixels, the image can’t be increased in size without the image blurring. An SVG however is suitable up and down without any distortion.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">UI</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">User interface is everything that a website user sees and interacts with while on the site. Everything from the buttons, pages, links, and so on.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">URL</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Uniform Resource Locator is a distinct identifier that’s used to locate resources and sites on the internet. The URL of the website you are currently on is ahdesign.website/post/web-development-dictionary. </span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">UX</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">User experience is the entire design process involved in creating meaningful, relevant, and user-friendly experiences for the end user. Lots of thought goes into UX because if something is too hard to be used, or rather obscene to look at, users don’t stay long.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Web app</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Web apps are similar to other types of software except for the part where the software runs solely on the internet. If you have ever used Telegram or WhatsApp Web in your browser then you have used a web app.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Whitespace</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Whitespace is any area on a webpage between other elements (images, text, etc) that is without anything there. It is typically used for aesthetic purposes and to make websites easier to look at and less clogged.</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Widget</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Have you visited a website and seen notifications or blocks of ads, typically on the side of the screen, well this is a widget</span></p><h4><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(102, 102, 102);">Wireframe</span></h4><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">Essentially just a blueprint of your website so it can be visualized.</span></p>
---
